after_at_head.dependency_note: The isolated ba1a1cf7 packet branch lacks generated/promoted E4 lane evidence. Two record tests have zero comparator records, the write-safety reverify test fails before the router because its node-gate file is absent, and the default reverify now selects the correct v4 config_id but reports missing L4 evidence. These are integration-time regeneration residuals after SP2 repairs the REGEN-PROMOTION-PIN-CYCLE; they are not SP7 scope, because SP7 only rebinds ER-PROGRESS-STALE-PINS in docs_tmp/phase_16/BB_ER_PROGRESS.json. The five /status expectation failures remain untouched for SP4.

residual_accounting.justification: The test filters comparator records to the accepted L4 lane, and the catalog declares this exact path while the ba1a1cf7 worktree does not contain it. scripts/e4_parity/oh_my_pi_p6_lane_projection.py builds the L4 comparator and lane_acceptance_artifacts.py dispatches that builder from run_lane. Taxonomy assigns SP2 the REGEN-PROMOTION-PIN-CYCLE and explicitly says its promotion/rebind write-set includes generated catalog bindings, claims, and evidence manifests. SP7 only owns phase_16 progress pins; F5 concerns P6.6 task/job artifacts, not P6.0-L4.

The pagination case requires more than one catalog-backed bb.e4.comparator_report.v1 record. The catalog declares these 21 comparator-role paths and zero corresponding comparator payloads exist in this clean worktree. Taxonomy assigns the generated evidence promotion/rebind cycle to SP2 and requires the fixed-point command after packet integration. This is broader regeneration accounting, not SP7 phase_16 pin rebinding; the P6.6 entry also depends on F5's canonical artifact before the post-F5 integrated rebind.

The test reads this exact file before making the API request and therefore fails before the SP3 router executes. scripts/e4_parity/oh_my_pi_p6_lane_projection.py:_node_gate constructs it and build_p6_lane writes it; lane_acceptance_artifacts.py invokes that producer through run_lane. SP2 owns the regeneration promotion cycle and the taxonomy requires fixed-point regeneration last. SP7 does not produce node-gate files, and F5 owns P6.6 rather than this P6.0-L4 lane.

stale_artifact_pins.claimed_producer_owner: F5 canonical artifact plus post-F5 integrated rebind

residual_accounting.justification: With SP3's v4 scope.config_id selected, validate_c4_chain reaches the correct freeze row and reports these exact missing L4 support artifacts rather than e4_configs.None. It also reports the separately recorded PIN_STALE ledger input; docs_tmp/phase_20/evidence/M1/f5_seed_digest_resolution.json establishes that F5 must produce the canonical ledger artifact, followed by integrated claim/evidence rebinding. scripts/e4_parity/oh_my_pi_p6_lane_projection.py builds the L4 evidence set under the lane_acceptance_artifacts/run_lane front door. Taxonomy assigns SP2 the promotion/rebind ordering defect and broad generated evidence write-set; SP7 remains limited to ER progress pins.

schema_lifecycle.details: All three authorized physical schemas now have canonical absolute IDs, exact pack membership, active family and lifecycle census rows, validating examples, and examples-manifest registrations. Convention suite: 16/16 passed; /v1/e4/schemas regression passed.

v4_claim_router.details: The handler dispatches bb.e4.support_claim.v4 to scope.config_id, retains top-level config_id only for supported v1-v3 claims, rejects unsupported versions or empty config IDs, and passes the string unchanged to validate_c4_chain. The intercepted focused regression observed oh_my_pi_p6_0_l4_mcp_browser_resource_v1.

full_api_suite.details: SP3 reduced the isolated suite from 10 failures to 9 by resolving the directly runnable health-schemas regression. The second router behavior is proven by the focused intercepted test; real reverification remains blocked by integration-time regeneration of the exact P6.0-L4 evidence listed in residual_accounting.

notes: The three registered families were already physically present and explicitly authorized by Phase 20 section 0.3; this packet completes their existing lifecycle and adds no fourth family. The existing TypeScript generator was run twice and produced byte-identical outputs, updating generated registry truth without TS repackaging. Worktree is clean at commit aea51859. Independent review is still required; implementer does not self-approve.
